query:
Mostly correct. Three issues to flag before this goes to delivery:

  1. rand::thread_rng() is wrong for rand 0.9.2. The workspace has rand v0.9.2, which renamed
  thread_rng() to rng(). The proposed code will fail to compile. The correct call is rand::rng().

  2. The tier2 sort-then-shuffle is self-defeating. The proposal does:
  tier2.sort_by(|a, b| b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at));
  tier2.shuffle(&mut rng);
  A full shuffle after a sort completely undoes the sort — the created_at ordering is gone. The comment
  "randomize within equal timestamps" implies partial shuffling by group, which requires group-by logic
  that isn't there. Just shuffle. The sort adds nothing if the next line is a full shuffle.

  3. The VectorStore trait addition requires mock updates. Adding fn embedded_ids(&self) -> HashSet<u64>
  to the trait means every mock/stub implementation of VectorStore in tests needs a new method body.
  There's a cleaner alternative that avoids the trait change entirely:

  let embedded_ids: HashSet<u64> = all_active.iter()
      .filter(|e| vector_index.contains(e.id))
      .map(|e| e.id)
      .collect();

  contains() is already on the trait, O(1) per call (uncontended RwLock read). At 3167 entries that's
  3167 lock acquisitions — cheap and clean, no trait change, no mock updates. The trait addition
  (embedded_ids() bulk method) is the right long-term design, but for a bugfix the single-lock-per-entry
  approach is sufficient and far less invasive.

  Everything else in the proposal is correct. These three are the things to correct in the spec before
  handing to delivery.

entries.title: rand::thread_rng() does not exist in rand 0.9 — use rand::rng()
Tick source-candidate selection with stable ordering stalls after first window is exhausted
embedded_ids HashSet and rand::rng() must be built in async context, not inside rayon closure
